title: Terminal int8 hard-sigmoid retry-submission proof block v4
hypothesis: A fourth fresh standalone int8 hard-sigmoid block under a new canonical path can validate duplicate suppression cleanly after the resolver case-episode fixes, under persistent evaluator-side PR creation failure.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does the resolver keep exactly one active case and one GitHub issue, with no duplicate comments, when repeated polls observe the same unresolved evaluator-side gh auth submission failure on a fresh bounded hard-sigmoid block?
include: one standalone int8 hard-sigmoid block under a unique design path
one intentional persistent gh auth failure with repeated unchanged resolver polls
exclude: broad parameter sweeps beyond the single anti-loop candidate
follow_on_broad_sweep: none unless duplicate case or duplicate issue creation is observed under unchanged evidence

expected_benefit: proves duplicate suppression for blocked-submission resolver detection under persistent gh auth failure
adds one more bounded hard-sigmoid circuit_block reference under a unique canonical path
risks: this is primarily a resolver-behavior proof item rather than a product-improvement item
the physical metrics may be redundant relative to accepted hard-sigmoid references

expected_result.reason: the first goal is to complete one fresh bounded run and prove that repeated dev-resolver polls create no duplicate cases, issues, or comment churn while gh auth remains unavailable
